cards.title: Output Configuration 1
cards.helpText: The cost for each output for your live channel is determined by a combination of bitrate, resolution, and frame rate for AVC outputs. The cost of AVC Enhanced VQ, and HEVC outputs is determined by a combination of resolution and frame rate. You can have a total of 6 unique output configurations per estimate. However, these unique configurations can be used across multiple channel types. For example, if you have 10 HD channels that have 4 SD outputs per channel and 10 SD channels with 3 SD outputs per channel that have the same specification, you would enter 70 SD outputs of that unique configuration (10 HD channels x 4 SD outputs = 40 SD outputs) + (10 SD channels x 3 SD outputs = 30 SD outputs) = 70 SD outputs. See the MediaLive pricing page for full descriptions and definitions.

components.text: The cost for each AVC output is determined by a combination of channel class, bitrate, resolution, and frame rate. The cost of  each AVC Enhanced VQ and HEVC output is determined by a combination of channel class, resolution and frame rate. A standard channel class includes two transcoding pipelines and single-pipeline channel includes one transcoding pipeline. You can create up to 6 unique output configurations per estimate.

components.label: Number of outputs
components.description: Total number of outputs across all channels. For example, if you have a 10 HD channels with 4 SD outputs on each channel, you will want to indicate 40 total SD outputs.

components.label: Active hours (On Demand)
components.description: Number of hours per month an output is in a running state.

components.label: Active hours (Reserved)
components.description: Number of hours per month an output is in a running state. 730 hours per month fixed for 1 Year terms.
